message:
chore(beads): tick ft-xbnl0.2.2 updated_at timestamp

Routine bead bookkeeping: single-line re-serialization of
ft-xbnl0.2.2 ("Collapse frankenterm-core runtime observation,
workflow, and maintenance loops onto Cx-first structured concurrency")
reflecting an updated_at tick. No status/notes/dependency changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -87,7 +87,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","title":"Collapse frankenterm-core runtime observation, workflow, and maintenance loops onto Cx-first structured concurrency","description":"# Goal
Move the core runtime hot path onto native Asupersync semantics.

This task exists because observation loops, workflow runners, maintenance paths, channels, locks, and sleeps are where the project’s long-lived behavior actually lives. If those remain Tokio-shaped or dual-runtime, the finish line is fake.","design":"# Design
1. Thread `&Cx` through owned async APIs in the core runtime hot path.
2. Replace detached spawning with scope-owned work and explicit region structure.
3. Replace legacy channel, lock, select, and sleep patterns with native Asupersync primitives.
4. Add deterministic tests for lifecycle, cancellation, backpressure, and shutdown-sensitive loops as the code moves.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Core runtime observation and workflow hot paths follow native Asupersync semantics.
2. The runtime no longer depends on Tokio-shaped spawn, time, or channel assumptions in the covered surfaces.
3. Deterministic tests exercise lifecycle, backpressure, and cancellation behavior.
4. Remote verification demonstrates that the migrated hot path still operates under real build and test conditions.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the heart of the migration. If this task is compromised by compat shortcuts, the rest of the finish-line story will remain weak.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the core `runtime_compat` hot path inside `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs` plus the observation/workflow/runtime consumers in `runtime.rs`, `events.rs`, `search_bridge.rs`, `watchdog.rs`, `telemetry.rs`, `restore_process.rs`, `session_retention.rs`, `survival.rs`, `retry.rs`, `wait.rs`, `workflows/mod.rs`, `workflows/runner.rs`, `workflows/engine.rs`, `caut.rs`, and `tui/query.rs`.

Native target shape: thread `&Cx` through owned async APIs, replace detached spawn with `Scope`, and replace Tokio-shaped channels/locks/time/select assumptions with native Asupersync semantics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.111137Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:18:04.462305Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","runtime","structured-concurrency"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.069062Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:01.387749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.111137Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1m7nk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.288225Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","title":"Migrate IPC, watch-daemon control sockets, and local control-plane streams to native Asupersync","description":"# Goal
Remove legacy async assumptions from the local transport layer that binds CLI, daemon, and watch-style control paths together.

This task exists because JSON-line IPC, local sockets, and related control streams are structural parts of the operator experience. They need native Asupersync ownership, not a migration seam that happens to pass tests today.","design":"# Design
1. Move accept loops, per-connection handlers, buffered reads and writes, and shutdown paths onto native Asupersync networking and structured concurrency.
2. Preserve protocol compatibility while changing the runtime semantics under the hood.
3. Make connection lifecycle and backpressure behavior deterministic and testable.
4. Fold any remaining compat use into explicit quarantine or remove it entirely.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Local control sockets and IPC code paths covered by this bead no longer depend on Tokio-like runtime helpers.
2. JSON-line protocol behavior remains stable while lifecycle semantics become native and structured.
3. Tests cover malformed input, concurrent clients, disconnects, and shutdown.
4. Remote verification is captured with exact commands and artifacts.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task is intentionally transport-focused. Higher-level command semantics belong to other beads.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the local control-plane transport slice currently still reached through `runtime_compat`, specifically `crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs`, `native_events.rs`, `event_stream.rs`, and related local socket/control-stream helpers. Inherited blocker `wa-16hou` remains relevant context, but ProudSalmon already completed a bounded `ipc.rs` shutdown-semantics support slice on 2026-04-01, so this lane should build on that work rather than redoing it.

Native target shape: Asupersync-owned Unix socket accept loops, buffered line I/O, structured per-connection task ownership, deterministic shutdown, and stable JSON-line protocol behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.503448Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:18:04.733570Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","ipc","watch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.481201Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:01.652596Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.503448Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","depends_on_id":"wa-16hou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.706674Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","title":"Replace TCP, TLS, and HTTP client or service boundaries with native Asupersync networking","description":"# Goal
Finish the network-facing runtime migration so service and client boundaries stop dragging Tokio-era dependencies into the system.

This task exists because TCP listeners, TLS handshakes, HTTP client work, and related service boundaries still define a large part of the remaining async debt. They also create transitive dependency pressure that blocks a true no-Tokio finish line.","design":"# Design
1. Replace direct TCP and TLS surfaces with native Asupersync networking where the project owns the boundary.
2. Replace outbound HTTP client usage with native Asupersync HTTP semantics.
3. Keep compatibility boundaries explicit and temporary if any framework cannot be removed immediately.
4. Add tests and benchmarks for handshake behavior, concurrent connections, and request latency so the migration is not justified purely by ideology.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Covered TCP, TLS, and HTTP client or service surfaces no longer require direct Tokio-era networking crates.
2. Any temporary compat boundary is isolated and named rather than living inside core business logic.
3. Verification covers correctness and at least basic performance non-regression.
4. Completion evidence records the exact remote commands and artifacts used to validate the cutover.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task absorbs the remaining network boundary work from older migration beads so the final no-Tokio gate is achievable.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the remaining network-facing async debt currently still pulled through `runtime_compat`, especially `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`, `web/server.rs`, `web/sse.rs`, and `web_framework.rs`. Inherited blockers `wa-1u55z` and `wa-3mfv9` are the old TCP/TLS/HTTP sub-ledgers now absorbed here.

Native target shape: native Asupersync TCP/TLS/HTTP boundaries with any unavoidable third-party compat surface isolated and named instead of living inside core business logic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.880960Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:18:04.999033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","http","networking","tls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.900530Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:01.905998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.880960Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u55z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.120057Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-3mfv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.333440Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","title":"Collapse runtime_compat quarantine and remove dual-runtime feature seams from core APIs","description":"# Goal
Shrink the compatibility seam down to what is genuinely unavoidable and remove dual-runtime logic from core project-owned APIs.

This task exists because a repo can claim Asupersync while still keeping Tokio-shaped abstractions alive in `runtime_compat`, feature flags, and helper APIs. That is migration debt, not a finished architecture.","design":"# Design
1. Identify which compat exports are still used by project-owned core logic after the preceding migration tasks.
2. Replace those uses with native Asupersync primitives or move them behind explicit quarantine adapters.
3. Remove dead feature branches and comments that describe a dual-runtime future that is no longer intended.
4. Leave only deliberately isolated boundaries, each with a named owner and removal path if it still exists.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Core project-owned APIs no longer rely on `runtime_compat` as a long-term abstraction layer.
2. Dual-runtime feature seams are removed from covered surfaces.
3. Remaining compat code, if any, is explicit quarantine with a named reason and owner.
4. The codebase narrative now matches the intended native-Asupersync architecture.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task is the architectural cleanup step that makes the migration real instead of forever in progress.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns final collapse of the `runtime_compat` quarantine after lanes `2.1` through `2.4` land, plus removal of direct runtime dependency declarations and dual-runtime feature seams that still exist in root/core/client/mux manifests.

Native target shape: core project-owned APIs no longer present Tokio-shaped abstractions; any surviving foreign boundary is explicit quarantine with a named reason and removal path.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:09.269713Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-15T07:26:55.415960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cleanup","compat","goal-line"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.543934Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:02.168169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:09.269713Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.750250Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.985345Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:32.225788Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:32.462956Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns final collapse of the `runtime_compat` quarantine after lanes `2.1` through `2.4` land, plus removal of direct runtime dependency declarations and dual-runtime feature seams that still exist in root/core/client/mux manifests.

Native target shape: core project-owned APIs no longer present Tokio-shaped abstractions; any surviving foreign boundary is explicit quarantine with a named reason and removal path.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:09.269713Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-15T07:42:03.973036Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cleanup","compat","goal-line"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.543934Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:02.168169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:09.269713Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.750250Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.985345Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:32.225788Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:32.462956Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","title":"Enforce zero tokio, smol, and async-io gates in supported builds, tests, benches, and docs","description":"# Goal
Turn the no-Tokio and no-Smol intent into a durable invariant.

This task exists because migration work is not closed until the repo actively prevents regression. Without hard gates, future contributions will quietly reintroduce runtime debt and undo the finish-line push.","design":"# Design
1. Add machine checks that fail when direct `tokio`, `smol`, or `async-io` usage returns in supported surfaces.
2. Extend doctor or audit surfaces so contributors can diagnose violations locally.
3. Sweep docs and comments so the narrative matches the final architecture.
4. Make the gate broad enough to defend shipping paths, tests, benches, and key docs without blocking legitimate quarantine that is explicitly owned.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Supported builds, tests, benches, and core docs are guarded against direct `tokio`, `smol`, and `async-io` reintroduction.
2. Violations fail in a way that is actionable for contributors.
3. Allowed quarantine cases, if any, are documented and intentionally exempted.
4. This bead provides the durable no-regression gate for the async migration lane.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the enforcement step that turns the migration into a maintained property rather than a one-time cleanup.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the permanent no-regression gate for the async migration, including manifest checks, source/doc sweeps, and explicit exemption handling for any named quarantine left after `ft-xbnl0.2.5`. The inherited final-cleanup bead is `wa-2h1ha`.

Guard scope should treat `runtime_compat.rs` as the only intentional temporary Tokio quarantine root during migration and fail fast if new direct `tokio`, `smol`, or `async-io` usage appears elsewhere in supported builds, tests, benches, or core docs without an explicitly documented owner.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:09.664604Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:56:58.474518Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T06:46:23.393672Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","ci","goal-line","quality-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:32.668868Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:07.246490Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:09.664604Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:32.944150Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","depends_on_id":"wa-2h1ha","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:33.208179Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2933,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.6","author":"jemanuel","text":"Closure (CC-1, 2026-04-15): Strict per-file allowlist gate enforced at three layers: scripts/check_no_runtime_regression.sh, crates/frankenterm-core/tests/ft_xbnl0_2_6_no_runtime_regression_gate.rs (3/3 pass), tests/e2e/test_ft_xbnl0_2_6_no_runtime_regression.sh (8-step scenario passes). 64 source/test/bench files quarantined under 5 named owners (ft-xbnl0.2.5, ft-xbnl0.2.6, ft-xbnl0.4.6, ft-22x4r, wa-2h1ha) - each entry carries owner+reason+removal_bead. runtime_compat.rs explicitly named as the only intentional Tokio quarantine root per design notes. Stale-allowlist check forces ratchet-down: as upstream beads migrate files, allowlist must shrink. Artifacts: /dp/frankenterm/tests/e2e/logs/ft_xbnl0_2_6_no_runtime_regression_20260415_062743/{summary.json,structured.log}, docs/ft-xbnl0-2-6-no-runtime-regression-validation.json (status=passed), docs/ft-xbnl0-2-6-no-runtime-regression-gate.json (canonical policy). Commit a72fb92c. Level A satisfied (unit+integration+fmt+clippy clean). Level B rch substrate refused offload (fail-open guard); local cargo test --no-default-features ran as fallback. Level C artifact bundle present. Closed under upstream blockers ft-xbnl0.2.5 and wa-2h1ha by design - this bead delivers the gate; those beads drive the allowlist toward zero.","created_at":"2026-04-15T06:56:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.3","title":"[EPIC] Supported-path capability completion and placeholder closure","description":"# Goal
Finish or honestly narrow every supported-path capability that still behaves like a placeholder, partial implementation, or misleading promise.

This epic exists because a swarm operator cannot trust a terminal or control-plane whose advertised pane lifecycle, alt-screen truth, scrollback visibility, detach semantics, renderer support, or mux-server operations still hide `todo!()` branches or fake state.","design":"# Design
1. Reuse the placeholder-remediation program where it already owns the surface.
2. Add explicit new tasks for gaps that are real but not yet represented cleanly, especially mux-server sessionhandler operations and any final supported-path sweep.
3. Prefer small honest capability surfaces over broad imaginary ones: either ship the behavior for real or narrow the contract in code, tests, and docs.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every supported-path capability covered by this epic is either implemented for real or explicitly narrowed with operator-visible semantics.
2. Safety-relevant state such as alt-screen, detach support, pane lifecycle, and scrollback availability is no longer based on placeholders.
3. Renderer and platform support claims match actual tested behavior.
4. Shared mux-server implementation no longer contains supported-path `unimplemented!()` branches for pane and window operations.","notes":"This epic is about truth. If a feature is intentionally unsupported, say so explicitly and prove callers can distinguish that from not implemented yet.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.194726Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:00.157989Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capability-truth","goal-line","placeholder-remediation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:52.194726Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.3.1","title":"Finish machine-control and client-domain truth needed for safe robot actions","description":"# Goal
Make robot and client-domain actions rely on real state instead of placeholders or stale assumptions.

This task exists because pane creation, alt-screen truth, and safety-sensitive input behavior are core to swarm orchestration. If those surfaces are not authoritative, the control plane is not trustworthy no matter how much else is implemented.","design":"# Design
1. Build on the existing client-domain placeholder-remediation work for remote spawn and alt-screen truth.
2. Ensure policy and machine-control paths consume the same authoritative state pipeline rather than side heuristics.
3. Make errors explicit when a capability is unsupported by design instead of letting raw placeholder behavior leak through.
4. Verify that robot-facing semantics stay coherent after the implementation changes.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Client-domain pane lifecycle and alt-screen truth are authoritative in the covered machine-control paths.
2. Policy-sensitive send and wait semantics consume real state rather than hardcoded fallbacks.
3. The covered surfaces no longer expose placeholder-quality capability behavior.
4. Verification evidence covers normal behavior, failure behavior, and safety-sensitive transitions.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task is the control-plane trust anchor for human and robot actions. It depends on truth, not just UI polish.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.2`): this lane owns finish-line machine-control/client truth and builds primarily on `ft-akx00.2.3` (remote `spawn_pane` + authoritative alt-screen) and `ft-akx00.5.4` (stale `PaneCapabilities` stub language). Earlier placeholder beads `ft-akx00.2.1`, `ft-akx00.2.2`, and `ft-akx00.2.4` are already closed and are no longer finish-line blockers.

 {"id":"wa-juvd","title":"Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring)","description":"# Task: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust)

## Goal
Add an MCP server entrypoint that can be enabled/disabled via feature flag and shares core logic with robot mode.

## Requirements
- Behind `--features mcp` (no extra deps for default build).
- Clean lifecycle:
  - init
  - register tools/resources
  - serve
  - shutdown
- Deterministic error handling + logging.

## Design notes
- Do not implement business logic in handlers; delegate to existing "robot" functions.
- Prefer a thin translation layer:
  - MCP params → internal args types
  - internal result → MCP result

## Deliverables
- `wa mcp serve` (or `wa serve-mcp`) command.
- Minimal server with one read-only tool (`wa.state`) wired end-to-end.

## Testing
- Build/feature tests:
  - `cargo check --features mcp` passes
- Integration tests (see `wa-nu4.3.1.5`):
  - server starts and responds to a basic tool call using fixtures/stubs
  - error mapping is stable (no panics)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo run --features mcp -- mcp serve` starts server without panicking.
- `wa.state` tool works end-to-end against a fixture/stub or a real WezTerm instance.

 {"id":"wa-k0td","title":"Crash loop detection + backoff","description":"## What
Detect repeated watcher crashes and apply exponential backoff.

## Why
Prevents restart storms and protects system stability.

## How
- Track crash count + timestamps
- Apply capped backoff before restart
- Emit structured crash-loop events

## Success Criteria
- Backoff increases on repeated failures
- Crash loop events are observable","notes":"Audited crates/wa/src/main.rs: crash-loop backoff + structured logging already present in HEAD (run_watcher_with_backoff, crash window/threshold, non-retryable lock errors). No code diff needed; baseline build errors remain (NotificationConfig missing email + rsplit_whitespace typo).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:12:43.174712436Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:47:25.615659Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:47:25.615668Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"wa-k0tk5","title":"Refactor native_events.rs to asupersync","description":"# Refactor native_events.rs to asupersync

## Background
native_events.rs (~400 LOC) listens for events from vendored WezTerm over a Unix domain socket and forwards them via mpsc channel.

## Current tokio usage
- tokio::net::UnixListener for WezTerm event socket
- tokio::io::BufReader + AsyncBufReadExt for line reading
- tokio::sync::mpsc channel for event emission
- tokio::spawn for background listener task

## Migration
1. Replace UnixListener with asupersync::net::UnixListener (via Cx)
2. Replace BufReader+AsyncBufReadExt with asupersync buffered I/O
3. Replace mpsc channel with asupersync::channel::mpsc (two-phase send)
4. Replace tokio::spawn with scope.spawn inside cx.region()
5. Replace any cancellation logic with cx.checkpoint()

## Unit tests to include
- Test event listener starts and accepts connections
- Test line-delimited parsing of WezTerm events
- Test channel delivery (reserve/commit) under normal operation
- Test cancellation: listener shuts down cleanly when scope exits
- Test reconnection: listener handles socket disconnect gracefully
- All tests use LabRuntime with seed-locked deterministic scheduling
- Tests include structured logging: connection events, parse events, channel sends

## Acceptance criteria
- Native event listener works on asupersync
- Line-delimited event parsing preserved identically
- Two-phase channel sends for event emission
- Clean shutdown via structured concurrency
- 5+ unit tests with LabRuntime

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Concurrent event processing testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to test concurrent event processing scenarios:
  - Multiple WezTerm instances connecting simultaneously — verify no event loss or cross-talk
  - Interleaved event parsing and channel sends — verify event ordering preserved per-source
  - Race between listener shutdown and in-flight event processing — verify no orphan tasks
  - Concurrent reconnection attempts — verify listener correctly handles rapid disconnect/reconnect cycles

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for event dispatch latency**: Add `benches/native_events.rs` measuring:
  - Event parsing latency: raw line → parsed event struct
  - End-to-end event dispatch latency: event arrives on socket → consumer receives via channel
  - Event throughput: events/sec for sustained streaming (100, 1000, 10000 events)
  - Channel backpressure impact: throughput degradation as channel approaches capacity
  - Comparison with tokio-based event listener for equivalent workloads","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:18:46.334926Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:52:16.929700Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-k0tk5","depends_on_id":"wa-1bznu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:19:00.288870Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-k0tk5","depends_on_id":"wa-2abzy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:19:00.199188Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-k0tk5","depends_on_id":"wa-q8vj3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:19:00.111837Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"wa-oud5","title":"MCP audit integration: record each tool call decision/outcome (redacted)","description":"# Task: MCP audit integration

## Goal
Ensure MCP usage is trustworthy by recording an audit record for each MCP tool call.

MCP is high-risk because it is typically invoked by an agent. We need:
- a trace of what was requested
- why it was allowed/denied
- what was actually executed

## Requirements
- For each MCP tool invocation:
  - record tool name
  - record a redacted summary of arguments
  - record policy decision and preconditions
  - record outcome (success/error) and elapsed time
- Link audit entries to any underlying action audit entries (send/workflow).

## Redaction
- Do not store secrets or full inputs.
- Store hashes/summaries consistent with the shared redaction system.

## Testing
- MCP tests (`wa-nu4.3.1.5`) must cover:
  - tool call emits an audit record on allow AND deny
  - redaction behavior on representative arg payloads
  - linkage between MCP audit record and underlying action audit record (when applicable)
  - reservation tool calls (`wa.reserve`/`wa.release`) emit audit rows

## Acceptance Criteria
- Calling an MCP tool always results in an audit entry, even when denied.
- Audit entries are sufficient to debug a failed MCP automation without reproducing.


LABELS: area-audit, area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ bd-4vx.3.8: Audit trail storage: audit_actions table + queries + retention/redaction hooks ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ bd-4vx.8.7: Audit trail emission: record allow/deny for every action (send, workflow steps, MCP) with redaction ● P0
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ bd-nu4.3.1.3: Implement MCP tools (full set) by delegating to robot/core APIs ● P1

 {"id":"wa-p3i","title":"E2E script: watch-and-notify mode (--notify-only events → notifications)","description":"# E2E script: watch-and-notify mode (`wa watch --notify-only`)

## Goal
Validate end-to-end that notify-only mode:
- detects events and emits notifications
- **does not auto-handle** (no workflows run, events remain unhandled)
- respects notify filters and throttling
- includes suggested actions in notifications

This is the integration proof for notify-only mode (`wa-spi`) + notifications (`wa-psm`) under the standard E2E harness.

## Key constraints
- Deterministic: **no fixed `sleep N`**. Use bounded waits (polling with timeouts) and a quiescence detector.
- Safe shutdown: watcher processes must be stopped gracefully and reliably (SIGINT/`wa stop`), with bounded waits.
- No global config mutation: use an isolated E2E workspace/config.
- Artifacts must be redacted (no transcript leakage).

## Test setup
- Start a harness-owned mock webhook server with:
  - `/received` to read captured notifications as JSON
  - `/attempt_count` (optional)
  - request logging to artifacts
- Start WezTerm mux + dummy panes that can deterministically emit known events.
- Start `wa watch` in notify-only mode in a dedicated E2E workspace:
  - `wa watch --notify-only …`
  - notifications configured to point to the mock server

## Scenarios

### 1) Notify-only mode does not auto-handle
- Start watcher: `wa watch --notify-only --workspace <e2e> …`
- Trigger a known event that would normally be auto-handled (e.g., compaction):
  - via dummy pane output (preferred) OR a harness `wa e2e emit` helper
- Wait until mock server has ≥1 received payload (poll with timeout).
- Assertions:
  - notification payload exists and references the correct `rule_id` / pane
  - `wa events --unhandled --format json` still lists the event (still unhandled)
  - `wa workflow executions` (or DB evidence) shows **no** workflow ran for that event

### 2) Notification filter
- Start watcher with a filter:
  - `wa watch --notify-only --notify-filter "usage_limit" …`
- Trigger two events:
  - one matching filter (usage limit)
  - one non-matching (compaction)
- Wait for quiescence (bounded).
- Assert mock server received exactly one notification, for the matching event.

### 3) Throttling in notify-only mode
- Configure a low throttle budget in the E2E config.
- Trigger a burst of N events quickly (deterministic dummy output).
- Wait for quiescence (bounded).
- Assert:
  - delivered notifications ≤ configured budget
  - watcher logs contain explicit “throttled” markers for skipped notifications

### 4) Suggested action included
- Trigger compaction.
- Wait for first notification.
- Assert the notification body includes a suggested remediation (e.g., `wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane <id>`).

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`) with prerequisites and default inclusion status.

## Artifacts
- `wa_watch_notify_only.log`
- `notifications_received.json` (redacted)
- `events_unhandled.json`
- `workflow_executions_slice.json` (or other “no workflow ran” evidence)
- `mock_server.log`

## Logging contract
Every step logs:
- case/scenario name
- watcher PID/workspace
- mock server bind address
- timeouts used

Example:
```
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] watcher started workspace=e2e pid=12345 notify_only=true filter=usage_limit
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] emit rule_id=codex.usage_limit_reached pane=3
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] wait received>=1 timeout=10s
[NOTIFYONLY_E2E] assert event still unhandled: OK
```

## Testing
- Determinism:
  - no fixed sleeps
  - all waits are “wait until condition” with explicit timeouts
- Correctness:
  - notification exists and matches expected `rule_id` + `pane_id`
  - event remains unhandled (no workflow execution)
  - filter and throttling behave as configured
- Shutdown reliability:
  - stop watcher via `wa stop --workspace <e2e>` when available, otherwise SIGINT + bounded wait
  - artifacts always include watcher exit status and last log lines

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Notify-only mode delivers notifications for detected events.
- [ ] Notify-only mode does not auto-handle (events remain unhandled, no workflows executed).
- [ ] Filters and throttling work deterministically.
- [ ] Notifications include suggested actions.
- [ ] Artifacts/logs are sufficient to debug failures.","notes":"Completed: added watch_notify_only scenario to E2E registry + harness (notify-only baseline, filter, throttling, suggested action checks) and updated e2e integration checklist. E2E script not executed here (requires WezTerm/mux).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderSnow","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:14:15.541602048Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:43:53.937055610Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:43:53.936888750Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"wa-p48pw","title":"Refactor DirectMuxClient (mux_client.rs) to asupersync","description":"# Refactor DirectMuxClient (mux_client.rs) to asupersync

## Background
mux_client.rs (997 LOC) is the most complex async module. Manages persistent Unix socket connections with custom binary protocol.

## Current tokio usage
- tokio::net::UnixStream for socket connection
- tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt} for framed I/O
- tokio::time::timeout for 5s connect/read/write timeouts
- tokio::sync::mpsc for pane output subscription
- tokio::sync::watch for cancellation signaling
- tokio::spawn + tokio::select! for background polling

## Migration order
1. Replace UnixStream with asupersync net
2. Replace AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt with asupersync I/O traits
3. Replace timeouts with Budget::deadline(Duration::from_secs(5))
4. Replace mpsc/watch with asupersync channels (two-phase send)
5. Replace tokio::spawn with scope.spawn for pane output subscription
6. Replace select! with asupersync::combinator::select for poll+cancel

## Critical: Wire protocol MUST NOT change
PDU framing: 4-byte big-endian length prefix + PDU type byte + payload.
The framing logic must produce identical wire format. This is non-negotiable.

## subscribe_pane_output() migration (hardest part)
```rust
// Becomes a scoped task:
cx.region(|scope| async {
    let (tx, rx) = asupersync::channel::mpsc::channel(capacity);
    scope.spawn(|cx| async move {
        loop {
            cx.checkpoint()?;  // Cancellation point
            let delta = fetch_pane_delta(cx).await?;
            let permit = tx.reserve(cx).await?;
            permit.send(delta);
        }
    });
    rx
}).await
```

## Comprehensive unit tests (LabRuntime + real sockets)
1. **Connect**: connect to mock Unix socket, verify stream established
2. **PDU round-trip**: write PDU frame, read back, verify bit-for-bit identical
3. **PDU partial read**: simulate slow writer, verify buffered reading assembles complete frame
4. **Connect timeout**: unreachable socket, verify Budget::deadline triggers Cancelled
5. **Read timeout**: connected but silent peer, verify read deadline triggers Cancelled
6. **Write timeout**: connected but full buffer, verify write deadline triggers Cancelled
7. **Pane output subscription**: mock server sends deltas, verify channel receives all
8. **Subscription cancellation**: cancel subscription mid-stream, verify no orphan tasks, no leaked obligations
9. **Two-phase channel safety**: cancel between reserve and send, verify no message loss
10. **Concurrent operations**: multiple requests over same connection, verify serialization
11. **Error recovery**: simulate broken pipe, verify appropriate error propagation

Each test logs: connection events, PDU encode/decode, timeout triggers, channel reserve/commit, scope enter/exit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All DirectMuxClient ops work through asupersync
- PDU wire format bit-for-bit identical (regression test)
- Timeout behavior preserved (5s each)
- Pane output subscription uses structured concurrency
- 11+ tests covering all scenarios
- Obligation leak oracle passes for all tests

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Concurrent client operations testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to exhaustively test concurrent DirectMuxClient operations:
  - Multiple pane subscriptions active simultaneously — verify no cross-talk between subscription channels
  - Interleaved PDU reads and writes on the same connection — verify framing integrity
  - Concurrent connect attempts — verify connection serialization
  - Race between cancellation and PDU commit — verify no partial PDU writes

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for client operation latency**: Add `benches/mux_client_ops.rs` measuring:
  - PDU encode + write_all latency for typical payload sizes (64B, 1KB, 64KB)
  - PDU read_exact + decode latency for typical payload sizes
  - Full round-trip latency (send command PDU → receive response PDU) over Unix socket
  - Pane subscription setup latency (scope.spawn + channel creation)
  - Comparison with tokio-based DirectMuxClient for equivalent operations

## Cross-references

+{"id":"wa-p48pw","title":"Refactor DirectMuxClient (mux_client.rs) to asupersync","description":"# Refactor DirectMuxClient (mux_client.rs) to asupersync

## Background
mux_client.rs (997 LOC) is the most complex async module. Manages persistent Unix socket connections with custom binary protocol.

## Current tokio usage
- tokio::net::UnixStream for socket connection
- tokio::io::{AsyncReadExt, AsyncWriteExt} for framed I/O
- tokio::time::timeout for 5s connect/read/write timeouts
- tokio::sync::mpsc for pane output subscription
- tokio::sync::watch for cancellation signaling
- tokio::spawn + tokio::select! for background polling

## Migration order
1. Replace UnixStream with asupersync net
2. Replace AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt with asupersync I/O traits
3. Replace timeouts with Budget::deadline(Duration::from_secs(5))
4. Replace mpsc/watch with asupersync channels (two-phase send)
5. Replace tokio::spawn with scope.spawn for pane output subscription
6. Replace select! with asupersync::combinator::select for poll+cancel

## Critical: Wire protocol MUST NOT change
PDU framing: 4-byte big-endian length prefix + PDU type byte + payload.
The framing logic must produce identical wire format. This is non-negotiable.

## subscribe_pane_output() migration (hardest part)
```rust
// Becomes a scoped task:
cx.region(|scope| async {
    let (tx, rx) = asupersync::channel::mpsc::channel(capacity);
    scope.spawn(|cx| async move {
        loop {
            cx.checkpoint()?;  // Cancellation point
            let delta = fetch_pane_delta(cx).await?;
            let permit = tx.reserve(cx).await?;
            permit.send(delta);
        }
    });
    rx
}).await
```

## Comprehensive unit tests (LabRuntime + real sockets)
1. **Connect**: connect to mock Unix socket, verify stream established
2. **PDU round-trip**: write PDU frame, read back, verify bit-for-bit identical
3. **PDU partial read**: simulate slow writer, verify buffered reading assembles complete frame
4. **Connect timeout**: unreachable socket, verify Budget::deadline triggers Cancelled
5. **Read timeout**: connected but silent peer, verify read deadline triggers Cancelled
6. **Write timeout**: connected but full buffer, verify write deadline triggers Cancelled
7. **Pane output subscription**: mock server sends deltas, verify channel receives all
8. **Subscription cancellation**: cancel subscription mid-stream, verify no orphan tasks, no leaked obligations
9. **Two-phase channel safety**: cancel between reserve and send, verify no message loss
10. **Concurrent operations**: multiple requests over same connection, verify serialization
11. **Error recovery**: simulate broken pipe, verify appropriate error propagation

Each test logs: connection events, PDU encode/decode, timeout triggers, channel reserve/commit, scope enter/exit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All DirectMuxClient ops work through asupersync
- PDU wire format bit-for-bit identical (regression test)
- Timeout behavior preserved (5s each)
- Pane output subscription uses structured concurrency
- 11+ tests covering all scenarios
- Obligation leak oracle passes for all tests

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Concurrent client operations testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to exhaustively test concurrent DirectMuxClient operations:
  - Multiple pane subscriptions active simultaneously — verify no cross-talk between subscription channels
  - Interleaved PDU reads and writes on the same connection — verify framing integrity
  - Concurrent connect attempts — verify connection serialization
  - Race between cancellation and PDU commit — verify no partial PDU writes

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for client operation latency**: Add `benches/mux_client_ops.rs` measuring:
  - PDU encode + write_all latency for typical payload sizes (64B, 1KB, 64KB)
  - PDU read_exact + decode latency for typical payload sizes
  - Full round-trip latency (send command PDU → receive response PDU) over Unix socket
  - Pane subscription setup latency (scope.spawn + channel creation)
  - Comparison with tokio-based DirectMuxClient for equivalent operations

## Cross-references

 {"id":"wa-pfsj","title":"WAL recovery: automatic checkpoint and journal cleanup on startup","description":"# Task: WAL Recovery on Startup

## Goal
Handle unclean shutdown scenarios gracefully by recovering WAL/journal state.

## Problem
If wa crashes or is killed while writing:
- WAL file may be large (uncommitted transactions)
- Journal file may exist (transaction in progress)
- Next startup should recover cleanly

## Implementation

### Startup Recovery Sequence
```rust
impl StorageHandle {
    pub fn open_with_recovery(path: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
        // 1. Check for recovery situation
        let wal_path = path.with_extension("sqlite-wal");
        let journal_path = path.with_extension("sqlite-journal");
        
        if wal_path.exists() || journal_path.exists() {
            tracing::info\!(
                wal_exists = wal_path.exists(),
                journal_exists = journal_path.exists(),
                "Recovery situation detected, will attempt recovery"
            );
        }
        
        // 2. Open with recovery pragmas
        let conn = Connection::open(path)?;
        
        // 3. Run quick integrity check
        let result: String = conn.query_row(
            "PRAGMA quick_check",
            [],
            |row| row.get(0)
        )?;
        
        if result \!= "ok" {
            tracing::error\!(result = %result, "Database corruption detected");
            return Err(StorageError::Corruption { details: result });
        }
        
        // 4. Checkpoint WAL if large
        let (busy, log, ckpt): (i32, i32, i32) = conn.query_row(
            "PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(PASSIVE)",
            [],
            |row| Ok((row.get(0)?, row.get(1)?, row.get(2)?))
        )?;
        
        tracing::info\!(
            busy = busy,
            wal_frames = log,
            checkpointed = ckpt,
            "WAL checkpoint completed"
        );
        
        // 5. If WAL is huge, do full checkpoint
        if log > 10000 {
            tracing::warn\!(frames = log, "Large WAL detected, doing full checkpoint");
            conn.execute("PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)", [])?;
        }
        
        Ok(Self { conn })
    }
}
```

### Logging Contract
On recovery:
```
INFO  wa::storage: Recovery situation detected wal_exists=true journal_exists=false
INFO  wa::storage: Quick integrity check passed
INFO  wa::storage: WAL checkpoint completed busy=0 wal_frames=5432 checkpointed=5432
INFO  wa::storage: Database recovery complete
```

On corruption:
```
ERROR wa::storage: Database corruption detected result="malformed disk image"
ERROR wa::storage: Cannot recover automatically, run: wa db repair
```

## Testing
- Unit tests: mock WAL/journal scenarios
- Integration: kill wa mid-write, verify recovery on restart
- E2E: bd-4vx.10.12 (workflow resume after restart) covers this path

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unclean shutdown followed by restart recovers automatically
- Large WAL files are checkpointed on startup
- Corruption detected and surfaced clearly
- Recovery actions logged for debugging
